digest:
Knowmarks is an open-source, local-first knowledge management system designed to serve as a shared substrate for humans and AI agents. It ingests a wide variety of content types—URLs, PDFs, Office documents, YouTube videos, GitHub repositories, Readwise highlights, browser clips, AI conversation threads, email, and local files—into a single, format-agnostic corpus. The system organizes this corpus through three ownership-clean surfaces: user-authored Notes, an LLM-compiled Wiki (proposed), and a pipeline-extracted Graph (proposed). Spaces unify the previous concepts of projects, clusters, and curated collections into a single composable type with attributes for origin, membership rule, and lifetime intent.

Retrieval is optimized for agents via an MCP server that employs a surface cascade, prioritizing wiki articles for token efficiency, followed by graph traversal and passage-level retrieval. The system supports space-scoped queries with token budgets and citation grounding. Humans interact with the knowledge base through a Conversational Companion surface, a web dashboard featuring a knowledge pulse, CLI tools, a REST API, a Chrome browser extension, and a macOS desktop app. The tech stack is built on Python 3.11+, SQLite, and FastAPI, with pluggable embedding providers and a front-end built with Vite and TypeScript.
